explanation: The original command line matches the Sigma rule exactly and is included as a baseline for testing.
The equals sign (=) is used instead of a space to separate the flag and value, which is a valid syntax substitution.
The order of the flags is changed, which is allowed if the program accepts arguments in any order.
Both flags use the equals sign (=) syntax for argument assignment, which is a valid substitution.
An additional -- is appended to the command line, which is a safe insertion that terminates option parsing in many programs.
An extra flag is added that is ignored by the program, which is a safe insertion that does not affect detection.
The long option --Module is changed to a short option -Module, assuming the program accepts this substitution.
The executable name is modified to lowercase, which may be a valid bypass if the detection rule is case-sensitive.
